title: physaria goodrichii
intro: Physaria goodrichii, also known as Goodrich's bladderpod or Goodrich's rock-cress, is a rare, annual or biennial flowering plant that belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It is native to the southwestern United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. Physaria goodrichii can be prepared for herbal use by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or making a tincture from the plant's roots and stems.
